003 * contributor license agreements.  See the NOTICE file distributed with
004 * this work for additional information regarding copyright ownership.
005 * The ASF licenses this file to You under the Apache License, Version 2.0
006 * (the "License"); you may not use this file except in compliance with
007 * the License.  You may obtain a copy of the License at
008 *
009 *      http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0
010 *
011 * Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software
012 * distributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS,
013 * WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.
014 * See the License for the specific language governing permissions and
015 * limitations under the License.
016 */
017package org.apache.juneau.marshall.jena;
018
019/**
020 * Subclass of {@link RdfParser} for parsing TriG into POJOs.
021 *
022 * <p>
023 * Accepts RDF datasets in TriG format (Turtle with named graphs) and converts them to Java beans,
024 * maps, collections, and primitive types. Date/time literals are parsed via ISO 8601.
025 *
026 * <h5 class='figure'>Examples:</h5>
027 * <p class='bjava'>
028 *    <jc>// Parse TriG string into a bean.</jc>
029 *    String <jv>triG</jv> = ...;  <jc>// TriG content</jc>
030 *    Person <jv>person</jv> = TriGParser.<jsf>DEFAULT</jsf>.parse(<jv>triG</jv>, Person.<jk>class</jk>);
031 * </p>
032 * <p class='bjava'>
033 *    <jc>// Or use the TriG marshaller for convenience.</jc>
034 *    Person <jv>person</jv> = TriG.<jsm>to</jsm>(<jv>triG</jv>, Person.<jk>class</jk>);
035 * </p>
036 * <p class='bjava'>
037 *    <jc>// Custom parser with swaps.</jc>
038 *    TriGParser <jv>p</jv> = TriGParser.create().swaps(DateSwap.<jk>class</jk>).build();
039 *    MyBean <jv>bean</jv> = <jv>p</jv>.parse(<jv>triG</jv>, MyBean.<jk>class</jk>);
040 * </p>
041 *
042 * <h5 class='figure'>Example input (bean with name/age):</h5>
043 * <p class='bcode'>
044 *    @prefix j: &lt;...&gt; .
045 *    &lt;...&gt; { &lt;...&gt; a j:Person ; j:name "Alice" ; j:age 30 . }
046 * </p>
047 *
048 * <h5 class='figure'>Complex (nested address + array):</h5>
049 * <p class='bcode'>
050 *    @prefix j: &lt;...&gt; .
051 *    &lt;...&gt; { &lt;...&gt; a j:Person ; j:name "Alice" ; j:age 30 ;
052 *       j:address &lt;.../address&gt; . }
053 *    &lt;...&gt; { &lt;.../address&gt; a j:Address ; j:street "123 Main St" ;
054 *       j:city "Boston" ; j:state "MA" . }
055 * </p>
056 *
057 * <h5 class='section'>Notes:</h5><ul>
058 *    <li class='note'>This class is thread safe and reusable.
059 * </ul>
060 *
061 * <h5 class='section'>See Also:</h5><ul>
062 *    <li class='link'>{doc jmr.RdfDetails}
063 * </ul>
064 */
065@SuppressWarnings({
066   "java:S110" // Deep inheritance inherent to the RDF parser hierarchy.
067})
068public class TriGParser extends RdfParser {
069
070   /** Default TriG parser, all default settings.*/
071   public static final TriGParser DEFAULT = new TriGParser(create());
072
073   /**
074    * Creates a new builder for this object.
075    *
076    * @return A new builder.
077    */
078   @SuppressWarnings({
079      "java:S1452" // Builder<?> wildcard return intentional; callers chain via fluent API without needing the concrete type
080   })
081   public static RdfParser.Builder<?> create() {
082      return RdfParser.create().triG();
083   }
084
085   /**
086    * Constructor.
087    *
088    * @param builder The builder for this object.
089    */
090   public TriGParser(RdfParser.Builder<?> builder) {
091      super(builder.triG().consumes("application/trig"));
092   }
093}
